pattern: Greedily force every row to start with a one (the most valuable bit), then flip each remaining column to whichever orientation gives it more ones, and sum the resulting binary rows.
hints: The leading bit of each row is worth more than all the others combined, so every row should be made to start with a one.
Once the first column is all ones, flipping any other column is independent: pick the orientation of that column that maximizes its count of ones.

For each row, if its first entry is zero toggle the whole row. Then for each remaining column count the ones; if fewer than half are set, toggle that column. Finally interpret each row as binary and add them up.

edge_inputs.description: first example: three by four grid
second example: single zero cell flips to one
single one cell needs no flips
rows already leading with one but a column should flip
all zeros: every row flips, then columns settle
tie in a column with even row count keeps it as-is after the flip rule
single column forces all rows to one

stress.description: random binary matrices of varying shape up to the size bound
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    m = rng.randint(1, 10)
    n = rng.randint(1, 20)
    grid = [[rng.randint(0, 1) for _ in range(n)] for _ in range(m)]
    return [grid]
stress.note: rows capped at ten so the 2^m brute oracle stays fast
